desc:
Some sort of disaster to the west has severely damaged this passage. To the west
the walls are riddled with fractures and tiny pits, whose frequency decreases as
one moves east. A narrow stairway leads down to the deeper portions of Moria --
it also appears to head slightly south as well.
name: Crumbling Passage

desc:
This small chamber has been victimised by a massive explosion of some sort --
too many years have passed to determine if this was done by fell magic or by
some fiery device of the orcs. The stone of both floor and walls is covered with
fractures, and the rock of the ceiling looks to have been slightly melted by
great heat. A passage leading to the west has been completely sealed by debris,
but it is possible to proceed to the east. There is a small inscribed plaque set
in the wall, but it too has been damaged.
name: Shattered Chamber

desc:
Plunging into blackness, these ancient stairs have been undisturbed for ages.
Each step is coated with a thick layer of dust and cobwebs often stretch
completely across the vertical shaft through which the stairs pass.
name: Hidden Stairs

desc:
Both above and below, these narrow stairs continue their journey. The layers of
accumulated dust bear no sign of footprints, so these stairs have probably lain
unused since the time when dwarves last inhabited these tunnels.
name: Hidden Stairs

desc:
A flight of stairs commences here, heading downward to the lower levels of
Khazad-dum. This passageway has suffered extensive damage, with small piles of
rubble from collapsed portions of the ceiling and walls cluttering the floor. To
the east, the corridor continues briefly, but then becomes totally closed off
due to a collapse of the entire roof. The passage also continues to the north.
name: Hidden Stairs

desc:
This passage has suffered heavy damage -- probabably in the tumults that fol-
lowed the waking of Durin's Bane. The masonry of the walls and ceiling has been
severely fractured, and in several place it has collapsed completely, revealing
the dark native rock behind. To the east there is a door that has somehow
survived, and the passage continues to the south. Almost unnoticed in the rubble
on the floor is a small metallic plaque that probably fell from the wall years
ago.
name: Rough Passage

desc:
This rough passage ends abruptly in a wall to the west. The masonry of the walls
and floor has cracked and fallen in several places, giving this area a rough and
shoddy look not usually found in dwarven constructions -- perhaps the tremors
that shook the earth after the Balrog was awakened proved too much for the stone
to handle.
name: Rough Passage

desc:
To the south the passage has been totally destroyed, but to the north it is
still possible to weave a path among the rocky debris. There are many spider
webs covering the rocks and walls, and they get thicker to the north.
name: Ruined Passage

desc:
The passage is totally choked with sticky strands of spider web. No longer mere
cobwebs, these threads grab and hold anything with which they come in contact,
forcing one to briefly fight free. The webs are noticeably thicker to the north,
while they abate somewhat to the south.
name: Webbed Passage

desc:
The webs are very thick here, and there are the dessicated remains of several
bats and rats strung into the threads. A shaft begins directly above -- it seems
to be the home of many spiders judging from the amount of webs visible. The
shaft must lead to some sort of food source for these arachnids, but it is
impossible to negotiate the climb required to discover its destination.
name: Dead End

desc:
These stairs have lain unused for ages, judging from the quantity of undis-
turbed dust that greets each falling footstep. Spiralling down a smooth-cut
shaft, the stairs approach a passage of some sort below, while they disappear
above into the dark.
name: Hidden Stairs

desc:
This flight of narrow stairs continues its winding journey both above and below.
There is a small landing here, and to the south the faint silhouette of a door
can be seen. The steps were obviously cut by the dwarves for they are suited to
the short stature of that people. There is a small plaque set in the northern
wall opposite the doorway, and runes are visible upon it.
name: Hidden Stairs

desc:
A very narrow set of stairs that lead down. A thick door made of stone is set in
the wall to the west. Judging by the weight of the door, this staircase was
rarely used.
name: Hidden Stairs

desc:
High on the eastern wall of this room, a wide shaft leads upwards toward the
open sky, and the ventilation provided has robbed this room of the musty smell
that years of neglect have imparted to other areas of Khazad-dum. By both the
large door on the western end, and the much smaller door on the eastern side,
many bones are lying, and among them are broken swords and axe-heads, and cloven
shields and helms. Many of the swords are crooked orc scimitars with blackened
blades. There are many recesses cut in the rock of the wall, and nestled within
are the broken remnants of large iron-bound chests of wood. In the centre of the
room, there is a large table cut of a single block of stone.
name: Chamber of Mazarbul

desc:
The passage to the west has been deliberately blocked with rubble, though a
narrow area has been left free to permit one to exit that way. A small wire cage
has been constructed in the southeastern corner -- it is currently empty. Thrown
on top of the cage are the bodies of several dozen small rodents and bats, all
with several dozen puncture wounds. Lying next to the cage is a small box
holding a set of twelve wicked-looking darts.
name: Burzruth Guardroom

desc:
Once home to a cadre of elite Dwarven guards, this large room is now almost
totally empty. The walls and floor are made of large sturdy blocks of granite
and are coated with a light layer of dust. From examining tracks, it is readily
apparent that this room sees occasional traffic still, though the cobwebs
gathered in the corners speak of a long abandonment.
name: Southern Guardroom

desc:
Judging by tracks in the grime on the floor, this passage must occasionally see
east-west traffic. The road leading south, however, appears to have been
undisturbed for ages. To the east the passage has deliberately been blocked by a
pile of rubble, though a small space wide enough for one to pass sideways has
been left against the northern wall.
name: Southern Cross

desc:
Covered with dust and guano, this road has lain fallow for many years. Even the
orcs seem to have totally ignored it, for there is no sign of their having been
here at all. To the north is some sort of intersection, but the darkness to the
south precludes any observation of the road ahead.
name: Southern Road

desc:
The sticky filth covering the floor is much thicker here -- so thick that one's
boots sink for a moment before finding the stone of the floor. There seems to be
a large chamber to the south, and from that direction comes a slight hint of a
rustling sound, too faint to be accurately identified.
name: Southern Road

desc:
This high-chambered vault is apparently the home of a large group of bats, for
the powder of old guano is inches deep throughout the chamber. Rustling can be
heard in the darkness above, but nothing can be seen in the inky blackness
overhead. To the east a flight of stairs leads up, and the smell of fresh air is
carried by a breeze originating in that direction.
name: Southern End

desc:
This small room is set high on a mountainside and provides an unobstructed view
of the lands of Rhovanion to the east. Probably used by the dwarves to keep
watch on the eastern approaches to Khazad-dum, this watchroom now provides
access to the outside world for the large population of bats in the chamber to
the west.
name: Watchroom

desc:
A wide ramp leads upwards at a gentle slope and probably at one time provided a
method of transporting heavy loads to the level above. Closer examination
reveals bits of mechanical rubble that was at one time a winch of some kind, but
there is not enough left to know for sure.
name: Bottom of Ramp

desc:
This grey corridor is fairly nondescript, apparently designed with an eye toward
function and not decor. Shoved to one side is a metal cart, similar to what one
might expect to find in a mine, and a few metres of rusted chain too brittle to
be of any use to anyone.
name: Accessway

desc:
Small corridors lead to the north, east, and west, while to the south is a small
duct from which a chilly breeze originates. The floor and walls are all done in
mundane grey stone and layered with the dust of ages.
name: Intersection

desc:
There is very little room to manoeuvre here, as this duct was not meant to be
used as a route of travel. A steady breeze from the area to the south brings a
noticeable chill to the air and keeps dust from settling on the stone of the
walls and floor.
name: Cramped Duct

desc:
This tiny space hardly merits the name 'room', but it is a good deal more
spacious than the narrow ducts leading north and west. A narrow shaft is cut in
the low stone roof, and from this shaft a fresh breeze is blowing.
Unfortunately, this shaft is far too small to permit anything larger than a rat
to proceed.
name: Ventilation Room

desc:
A fresh breeze wafts down this duct from the east, chilling the hard stone that
lines this area. Crawling seems the only feasible way to proceed, and it is
possible to do this both east and west.
name: Breezy Duct

desc:
This small duct is carpeted with a fuzzy layer of mould. To the west, this
passage empties into a chamber, and it is possible to continue crawling to the
east.
name: Mouldy Duct

desc:
This room is a veritable arboretum of underground vegetation -- all manner of
lichens and fungi cling to the stone of both wall and floor. Somehow these
plants have managed to prosper here in the darkness and have over the passing
centuries carpeted the room with life. This has not been without price, for the
mortar joining the stones of the walls has been made weak. A portion of the
western wall has collapsed into to the room, revealing a narrow crack extending
back into the mountain. A duct of some type is set in the eastern wall, and it
appears large enough for an armoured man to crawl through.
name: Fungus Room

desc:
This passage has a distinct musty odour, like bread that has been left outside
for days. The odour unmistakably grows stronger to the south, but there is no
hint as to its source.
name: Musty Passage

desc:
Once a storehouse for foodstuffs needed to feed a large population of dwarves,
this place was long ago pillaged of anything of use. There are pegholes cut into
the wall where once a system of shelves was set, and small bits of ancient
splintered wood lying about are all that remain of what must have once been
large storage bins.
name: Ancient Larder

desc:
The remnants of several ancient casks and vats provide a clue as to the use of
this area in ages past. Scattered about in the thick dust are rusted hoops of
iron, once part of large barrels perhaps, and other small bits of metallic
paraphernalia too corroded to be accurately identified.
name: Brewery

desc:
Totally abandoned for centuries, the only sign of life in this hall are the
scattered rat droppings and rodent tracks in the layers of dust. To the west,
large metal hinges hang loosely from the wall -- there is no sign of the door
which they once held. In that direction lies a chamber, while to the east is a
small intersection.
name: Unused Hallway

desc:
A steady brisk breeze from the south brings a chill to the air of this place.
There is little dust here, for the wind, though light, is strong enough to
prevent it from settling to depths found in some of the other abandoned halls.
There is an intersection to the south, and to the north the passage continues.
name: Chilly Corridor

desc:
Great piles of rags and furs are stacked here to form an extremely large pallet
stretching the entire length of the western wall. The floor is stained with
blood, both clotted and fresh, and there are piles of gnawed bones lying
haphazardly about. To the north and south narrow passages stretch -- long
abandoned judging by the stone rubble that almost completely blocks them. To the
east, an ancient wooden door hangs loosely from iron hinges, tenaciously
refusing to surrender and fall to the floor.
name: Troll Lair

desc:
The wide flat stairs pause here at a landing before continuing both up and down.
Piles of bones are strewn about, often cracked open and bearing the scars of
great teeth-marks. From the west comes a an odour reminiscent of unwashed bodies
and rotten meat, mingled together and thickening the air.
name: Mustering Stair

desc:
This wide and tall tunnel has been worn away by many years of heavy traffic and
abuse. A set of deep grooves has been chipped into the floor by the tramp of
boots and grind of stone on stone, and the tunnel walls and roof are covered
with scrawlings and scratches of all kinds. The tunnel turns south here into a
small and smoky chamber, and runs west towards the source of some shouting.
name: Well-used Tunnel

desc:
A small cave has been hewn square and fitted with benches and a sturdy irondoor
set in the west wall to serve as a guarded gateway into the Durghash's caves.
There is a small smoking fire in one corner which casts a feeble glow on the
surroundings here revealing some tattered wolfskins, a crude metal drum and
spoiled food scattered about on the ground. A large passageway begins to the
north and just to the east, another small cave beckons.
name: The Slaves' Door

desc:
This small foursquare room was probably originally used by the Moria dwarves as
a storage room for mining equipment and ores, but the Durghash have decided to
use it for more important purposes. The walls are honeycombed with cleverly
constructed racks once used to store tools, but which are now filled with sharp
knives, whips, irons and other pain-inflicting tools used by the orcs who have
come to love torture. There is a large firepit in the cave's centre which is
currently heating branding irons used in ritual scarmaking, slave torture or
simple murder, whichever the orcs here desire most. A sturdy metal cagedoor is
centred in the north wall, and a stone arch opens into a chamber to the west.
name: Cave of Scars

desc:
Many different veins of various ores and stones are embedded into the stone
walls of this cave. It is as if all of the basic elements have collided at this
nexus point under the mountain to form an underground rainbow showing all of the
beauty possible in stone. Of course, the Durghash have done their best to rob
the most valuable pockets of their ores, but so far the sheer bounty of Arda has
outstripped their greed. This magnificent junction runs east and west into large
open caverns and south into a smaller chamber.
name: Cave Junction

desc:
This huge place is just one part of an immense cavern which continues far into
darkness east and south of you. The cavern roof tapers down from some vast
height to join into the north wall, forming a large tunnel far to the west. The
air here seems to swirl and eddy, alive with silent motion and perhaps deadly
intent. There are a few scrapes and scratches on the walls, and maybe a faint
trace of old blood on some boulders here and there just within sight.
name: A Gigantic Cavern

desc:
Two gargantuan rock slabs meet here in the northeast corner of this cavern,
which is surely one of the largest in all Moria. The cavewalls stretch from this
point far up into the darkness overhead and seem to race towards some black
infinity both west and south. The floor is riven by many large fissures which
belch flames and sulphurous gases, probably resulting from some kind of volcanic
vent running under the rock here. The fires and gases produce a strange rhythmic
cacophony, almost a kind of speech from molten powers of Arda which must have
found a home here long before the Dwarves came.
name: A Gigantic Cavern

desc:
A small natural passage slopes downward, continues several paces east then ends
abruptly in a cul-de-sac. Apparently, there is nowhere to go other than back
towards the cavern to the west.
name: Cul-de-sac

desc:
The southeast section of this grand cavern is filled with many natural
stalagmite pillars which appear to burst upwards from the sandy soil of the
floor below. These sharp rocky formations are slick with dripping water which
feels warm to the touch, precluding thoughts of climbing upward to gain a better
view. The sandy soil shows signs of recent passage and some wide grooves as
well, and one cannot help but notice several big holes in the loose soil near
the southern wall. The cavern continues to the west and north.
name: A Gigantic Cavern

desc:
This part of the huge cavern appears to be its lowest section, and is partially
filled with slime-covered dark water which trickles in from the east. The black
water fills the southern portion of this place, but you cannot judge its depth
near the south wall where it seems to be deepest. A muddy shore surrounds the
black lake to the north and east, and from there you can see that the water does
indeed support some kind of life.
name: A Gigantic Cavern

desc:
This perfectly round chamber has been carefully chiselled from the smooth grey
stone by Dwarven hands. Four small tunnel openings were initially made in the
walls, but the ones to the east and west have been sealed off with tons of
collapsed grey stone. The oblong portal north leads into a dark cave, while the
rectangular portal south opens onto a large ascending staircase. Some runes in
the Angerthas Moria style have been carved into the smooth stone just below the
roof of the chamber.
name: The Round Chamber

desc:
A steep flight of small stone steps curves downward from west to north. The
walls here are covered with some defaced murals which depict laughing dwarves
sitting in piles of gems. Many of the Dwarven images have been hacked out of the
wall, apparently in spite of their creators.
name: Some Stone Steps

desc:
From inside this cramped, filthy pen, the outside world seems ten thousand
leagues distant. The roof is so low that even a young dwarf could not stand
erect in here, and there is no area that is cleared of sharp rock and bones
which could serve as a seat. There are many small piles of bones around, most
gnawed beyond recognition or smashed into tiny pieces. To the south, a fired
metal cagedoor with a sturdy lock bars any escape.
name: Slave Pen

desc:
A cramped and rock-filled tunnel leads a short distance from the main cavern and
ends just north at a smooth solid rockwall. Several sharp boulders jut out from
the walls and roof overhead, threatening to slash pieces from any creature that
would try to run past them. Only the tight passage which threads to the south
leads out of this place.
name: Cul-de-sac

desc:
The tunnel twists and turns, bending erratically from the north to the west
where it ends at a large and very hot cavern. There are many tracks in the sandy
soil on the tunnel floor, a good indication that this tunnel is often used
heavily by the Durghash.
name: Wide Twisting Tunnel

desc:
This wide tunnel connects to the Forge Door several paces east of here, and
bends several times before finally plunging downward and into the south. The
passage is littered with rotted food scraps and torn fur, and the walls are
covered with crudely scratched pictures and other orkish obscenities.
name: Wide Twisting Tunnel

desc:
This place is a medium-sized rough-hewn cell which has been equipped with stone
benches, a small stoneblock table and a crude metal drum. The most prominent
feature here is the sturdy irondoor centred in the north wall, upon which a
large lidless Redeye rising over an anvil has been painted. Dim light is
provided by five torches set horizontally in holes cut into the walls at regular
intervals. Possible exits are north through the irondoor, west into a wide
tunnel or east into another smaller chamber.
name: The Forge Door

desc:
The Lurg leaders of the patrol which mans the Forge Door have made this small
cave their barracks. The walls are covered by huge black warg furs, giving the
illusion of a vast blackness engulfing everything here. There is a set of three
giants' skulls set against the wall in the northwest corner, probably used as
secure storage by the greedy Lurgs. Several abandoned weapons lean up against
the west wall, within quick reach of anyone here. The only obvious exit is back
through the opening to the west.
name: Lurg Cave

desc:
The wide tunnel makes a few detours to the north and south, but still travels
generally east to west. The twists and turns cannot hide the steep descent to
the west, but they make it difficult to remain sure of the exact direction of
travel. This particular section of passageway is dark and quiet now, however a
close inspection of the walls and floor reveals many scrapes on the rocks here
which seem to indicate recent use of this tunnel.
name: Wide Twisting Tunnel

desc:
A sharp east-south curve has been hacked from the black rock of the mountain's
underbelly. A few crude iron support bars have been driven into rock here,
barely succeeding in holding large, loose crags in place. There are scattered
bone fragments among the debris which clutters the passage here, and a faint
stench of decay greets all who round this bend.
name: Bend in the Sloping Passage

desc:
The footing and general condition of this tunnel are quite poor. Jagged rock
fragments jut out from the tunnel walls and large dips and cracks in the floor
make every step an adventure. Fortunately, the tunnel is very wide so you can
pick your way through the rougher spots with a little care and can avoid the
worst pitfalls. The tunnel climbs to the east and descends to the west from this
point.
name: Sloping Passage

desc:
This crude passage is large enough to allow a party of stone giants to pass
through comfortably. The floor here is marked by rough scrapes and gouges,
indicating that very heavy stones were dragged through by some brute power
recently. The passage rapidly descends into darkness to the west, and passes
through a jagged opening into a large hall just east of this spot.
name: End of the Sloping Passage

desc:
The rough passage curves north to east, climbing sharply to the north. Many
grooves have been worn into the rock here, possibly due to very heavy portage
through this bend. The black walls appear to have been smashed back to enlarge
this passage from some smaller tunnel, as there is a large amount of black slag
cluttering the floor near the base of the side walls.
name: Steep Bend in the Sloping Passage

desc:
The spacious passage climbs steadily from east to west at this point. There are
many crude scrawlings and drawings on the sidewalls, most depicting huge things
smashing other smaller creatures with large boulders, along with large hordes of
dark beasts circling a large red eye. Two huge skulls have been arranged under
this particular drawing, both with large holes smashed through their crowns. The
stench of decay lingers in the air here.
name: Sloping Passage

desc:
Here the tunnel suddenly and sharply bends east to south, descending steadily as
it curves south. There is the strong scent of decaying meat in the air, perhaps
due to an old corpse concealed behind a loose stone in the wall. The rock here
appears quite scratched and battered, as if by frequent heavy use.
name: Bend in the Sloping Passage

desc:
The large passage curves to the north and west from this point, so sharply that
one cannot see what lies just around the bend. A few shattered slabs of black
marble lie scattered about the tunnel here, along with broken bits of ancient
statuary probably left by those who looted the great Dwarven Halls. The walls
here are covered with illegible scrawl and crude drawings, debased in their
design and intent.
name: Sharp Bend in the Sloping Passage

desc:
This very wide passage turns east and south, exhibiting a slight upgrade as it
travels north. It is quite clear of all rubble and other debris, and provides
good access in both directions. Faint echoes of grunts and harsh laughter seem
to come from the somewhere to the south.
name: Turn in the Sloping Passage

desc:
This section of tunnel receives dim light from a chamber just to the south,
revealing a rough set of black stone walls and a very high tunnel roof. There
are several broken swords, clubs and spearpoints lying among broken bits of
stone and bone here, these items arranged into small piles near one side of the
wide tunnel. Loud cursing, shrieking and growling blares forth from the southern
end of this stretch of tunnel, a torchlit chamber which appears to be the centre
of some activity. The tunnel also slopes upwards to the north into the quiet
blackness of the underbelly of the Misty Mountains.
name: Sloping Passage

desc:
This roughly square chamber was hewn from the surrounding stone by the Durghash
to serve as a guarded checkpoint for any passage into their caves. Smoking
torches line the walls above several crude stone tables and benches which are
covered by dirty animal skins and filthy parchments. Many creatures lurk nearby,
and growls, grunts and screams give loud testimony to their vigorous activities.
A sturdy irondoor painted with a large Red Eye blocks passage to the south,
while other exits lead north, west and east.
name: The Trolls' Door

desc:
This wide and long cavern serves as a barracks for the troll allies of the
Durghash tribe. There are many long, thick slabs of dark marble which line the
floor of this place, each of which acts as a bunk for a troll. Many of these
creatures mill about in here, looking for someone to fight, something to eat or
perhaps both. A graveyard of old bones lies scattered about on the stony floor
here, along with many patches of hide and fur. Some large openings have been
smashed through the north, south and west walls of this long cave.
name: Troll Barracks

desc:
This large and filthy cave proves to be the residence of the large and filthy
troll leader of the Durghash allies. He has collected a large number of broken
skulls of his former rivals, each of which adds to his status of its owner. A
large slab of red granite directly in the centre of this round cave serves as
both a table and a bed for the troll leader, depending only upon his mood.
name: Olog's Cave

desc:
Here is a small and humid cave filled with small niches which contain different
species of fungi. It is much hotter and more humid in this cave than in others
nearby due to steam escaping from several small fissures in the rocky floor of
this place. The fungi appear to thrive in this environment and there are many
varieties all of which appear to be growing extremely quickly, filling most of
the available space here.
name: Fungus Cave

desc:
Here is a narrow irregular cavern running north to south currently used as a
barracks for fierce Durghash warriors hand-picked by Shaghosh. There are small
stoneblocks and large warg furs strewn about the cave, and you see a metal drum
placed near a great firepit dug into stone in the centre of the cave. Each of
the orcs has claimed space here by fencing off an area with broken bones and
weapons, many topped with skulls of orcs or other prey for added effect. There
is a loud clamour rising all around you here, as orcs love to scream and shout
boasts as they prepare to raid and kill the other tribes of Moria. There is a
small iron hatch centred in the caveroof, but there appears to be no way to
reach it and investigate further.
name: Durghash Warrior Barracks

desc:
This large depression forms the northern part of the Durghash barracks and is
home to the wolves that faithfully serve the tribe. There are huge piles of
shredded hides, furs and bone splinters which the wolves have made through
continual sharpening of fang and claw. Here and there, a bright scrap of cloth
provides evidence of more substantial meals that these wolves have devoured. The
rest of the guard cave continues to the south.
name: Wolves' Den

desc:
The northern end of the cavern has been blocked off by a large wall fashioned
from the tanned skins of six huge dark wargs. There are many symbols carved into
the walls here, some crude depictions of hated enemies, others of unknown
intent. Stacks of skulls and weapons line the walls here, and many tattered fur
hides of various creatures cover all the floor. There is a firering in the south
end of this place, lined with 10 huge cavetroll skulls.
name: Lurg Leader Chambers

desc:
A huge and heavy irondoor has been set into the north wall of the tunnel,
allowing the Durghash to restrict passage through their caves. Gouges and
scratches in the walls and floor here indicate years of very rough use by the
legions of orcs and trolls which live here. The tunnel continues south from this
point.
name: Wide Tunnel before an Irondoor

desc:
The wide, worn passage bends sharply east and north, skirting a large stony rock
outcropping which bulges from the west wall here. Sharp shards of shattered
black marble lie scattered about the tunnel floor, the remains of an enormous
wallpanel which was smashed to bits. Most of the debris has been swept to the
edge of the tunnel by recent traffic passing through this way.
name: Bend in the Wide Tunnel

desc:
Something has smashed a huge gap into the southern wall of the tunnel here,
opening a passage into a large cavern to the south. This damage has also
resulted in a severe cave-in to the east, where the tunnel is now completely
choked with rubble and stone. The wide tunnel continues west, where things
appear to be more stable and safe.
name: Wide Tunnel End

desc:
The Durghash have set aside this large cavern as a home for any trolls who will
assist them in their bid to rule Moria. Many massive stone slabs have been
gathered into this featureless place, apparently to serve as tables and bunks
for the current inhabitants here. Several huge trolls step forward to look you
over, and the largest among them gestures towards a large pile of skulls which
lies in the far corner.
name: Troll Barracks

desc:
This large cavern is filled with stone, carcasses and trolls! There are stacks
of decomposing corpses, most missing heads and limbs and smashed beyond
recognition, piled in the southern corner of the cavern. Many immense slabs of
stone have been scattered on the cavern floor, serving as crude benches or
tables upon which half-eaten corpses lie. Trolls mill about, bellowing at one
another, eating, fighting and just enjoying the chaos surrounding them here.
name: Troll Feasting Hall

desc:
A small chamber has been smashed into the hard stone by the brute power of stony
troll fists. The trolls have made this place as safe storage for the various
trinkets that they have collected from their victims' corpses over past few
months. Some bright troll has realised that the Durghash have stolen everything
not secreted away from their prying hands and eyes.
name: Trolls' Storage Cave

desc:
An earth tremor must have collapsed the tunnel here recently, leaving a huge
pile of grey stone boulders and tons of loose rubble which block any passage
north. It appears that someone has attempted to remove some of the rubble and
shore up a few of the weakest points in the remaining tunnel with cast-iron
supports. One large dark boulder in the east wall appears to be quite different
from the surrounding stones, perhaps it fell from the roof during the quake? The
remaining tunnel descends rapidly south towards a large metal door.
name: End of the Twisting Tunnel

desc:
This quirky passage makes a wide bend from west to north here, rising steeply as
it turns. There is a wide, sturdy irondoor embedded in the southern wall of the
tunnel, and some dim light spills out from the cracks surrounding its jamb. The
irondoor has a keyslot and peephole worked into it, but there are no clues to
indicate what lies on the other side.
name: Twisting Tunnel before an Irondoor

desc:
This cave is the repository for all of the skulls of victims sacrificed to the
Dark Lord by the Durghash tribe. The floor is quite uneven and very slick here
because the rock has been completely covered over by thick troll and giant
skulls worn smooth by time and use. Each of the walls is dedicated to skulls
from a particular race, and the 'orc wall' appears particularly full. There is a
black wall panel left bare in the west wall which must be the inner side of a
concealed door, and an archway made from Dwarven skulls in the north wall.
name: Cave of Skulls

desc:
The residence of the Durghash Shaman reflects the fear and power he wields over
the rest of the tribe. He owns a vast collection of evil-looking knives which
are hung like trophies on the walls here, as well as a huge pile of scalps and
skins which all orcs treasure. Pungent incense burns in a bronze brazier which
is located at the foot of a large stone pillar in the centre of this chamber.
There is a set of stone chests in the northeast corner of this room and a large
bed of bear furs in the opposite corner. The only exit from this place is
through a black arch in the south wall.
name: Durghash Shaman's Chambers

desc:
This Temple is known and feared as the centre of worship of the Dark Lord by his
forces in Moria. It is rumoured that He caused the Red Eye to open here, giving
the great gift of molten fire to the Durghash so that they might overcome all
who oppose Him and serve his will more capably. This place has been painted with
the blood of thousands of sacrificed captives, so that all the surfaces are now
a rusty reddish brown colour. A great black altar located near the centre of the
cave is the only item that is not stained red. This large temple is surprisingly
clean, indicating that the Durghash dispose of the remains of their victims by
casting them into the Red Eye to the west.
name: Temple of the Red Eye
